scope_note: QA Pisano Lift to Prime Squares — Wall-Sun-Sun Regularity cert [417]. Primary sources: Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.2307/2309169 (π(p^k) in {π(p^{k-1}), p*π(p^{k-1})} for odd prime p; rank of apparition α(p^k) in {α(p^{k-1}), p*α(p^{k-1})}; neither exception found among tested primes — the open question now called Wall-Sun-Sun); Sun, Z.-H. and Sun, Z.-W. (1992) 'Fibonacci and Lucas congruences and their applications' Acta Arithmetica 60(3) pp.253-261 (equivalence: p^2 | F_{p-(5/p)} iff π(p^2) = π(p) iff α(p^2) = α(p); primes satisfying this are now called Wall-Sun-Sun or Fibonacci-Wieferich primes; none known); McIntosh, R.J. and Roettger, E.L. (2007) 'A search for Fibonacci-Wieferich and Wolstenholme primes' Mathematics of Computation 76(260) doi:10.1090/S0025-5718-07-01955-2 (numerical verification: no Wall-Sun-Sun prime below 2*10^14). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): The QA T-step T(b,e)=(e,b+e), applied modulo p^2 (integer modulus, Theorem NT, no float), yields a Pisano period exactly p times the period mod p: (C1) pi(p^2)=p*pi(p) for all 15 primes <=47 (direct T-step computation; worst case p=47: pi(47^2)=47*32=1504, found within 6*47^2=13254 bound); (C2) Wall-Sun-Sun Fermat quotient w_F(p)=F_{p-(5/p)}/p mod p != 0 for all 45 primes tested (22 split <=491, 22 inert <=193, p=5 ramified): cert [416] guarantees p | F_{p-(5/p)}; cert [417] verifies p^2 does NOT divide F_{p-(5/p)}; equivalently w_F(p) (well-defined integer quotient) is non-zero; falsifiable: any prime with w_F(p)=0 is a Wall-Sun-Sun prime; none found in test set; (C3) Rank lift alpha(p^2)=p*alpha(p) for all 45 primes: check p^2 does NOT divide F_{alpha(p)} (equivalent to Wall question / rank-of-apparition lift); verified by computing fib_at(alpha(p), p^2) and checking non-zero; requires only alpha(p) T-step iterations mod p^2 (< 500 per prime); (C4) QA T-orbit mod p^2 has p 'sheets': at step alpha(p), first component of orbit = F_{alpha(p)} ≡ 0 mod p but ≢ 0 mod p^2 (from C3); at step p*alpha(p), first component = F_{p*alpha(p)} ≡ 0 mod p^2 (by definition of alpha(p^2)=p*alpha(p)); verified for 5 sample primes {11,3,5,41,7}. NOTABLE DATA: For p=193, F_{alpha(193)}=F_97 mod 193^2 = 193 (w-rank=1); for p=421, F_21=10946=421*26 exactly (w-rank=26); for p=251, w_F(251)=250=-1 mod 251; for p=41, F_{20}=6765, F_20/41 mod 41 = 1. The Wall-Sun-Sun Fermat quotient w_F(p) appears to be uniformly distributed in {1,...,p-1} (no systematic bias observed). EQUIVALENCE (Sun-Sun 1992): conditions (A) pi(p^2)=pi(p), (B) alpha(p^2)=alpha(p), (C) p^2|F_{alpha(p)}, (D) w_F(p)=0 are all equivalent; all FAIL for every tested prime, consistent with no Wall-Sun-Sun prime existing. CHAIN: cert [415] T mod p, period pi(p), encodes split/inert/ramified -> cert [416] alpha(p)|p-(5/p), rank of apparition, one formula -> cert [417] T mod p^2, period p*pi(p), Wall-Sun-Sun regularity; second-order precision. All pure integer arithmetic. All independently falsifiable (one Wall-Sun-Sun prime would flip C1, C2, C3 simultaneously). Derived 2026-06-14.
